title: Daily Briefing — Mar 18
headline: Extreme fear + mean reversion signals = time to sharpen the shopping list, not panic.
body: Right, so the big picture: SPY sits at $670.79, firmly below its 20 MA ($680.19) and 50 MA ($685.88), with Fear & Greed at 25 — Extreme Fear territory. Volatility is elevated (VIX 21.55, just below its 20 MA), but contracting, which historically sets up mean reversion plays rather than breakdowns.

Sector rotation shows Energy (XLE $58.51, above all major MAs) and Materials (XLB $49.52, above 200 MA but weakening) holding up, while Technology and Discretionary are sliding but showing early signs of improvement. Healthcare and Financials are lagging — XLV and XLF both below 20/50 MAs, with Financials risk elevated. Watch the canary: XLF at $49.56 is flirting with its 200 MA ($52.60), not a healthy sign for the economy.

Contrarian radar is flashing. Fear & Greed < 25 is rare; last time we saw this, it marked major bottoms (see 2020, 2022). Most big names (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, META) are trading below their 20 MAs — per rule R006, that's a trigger to exit longs or sit tight. Abundance mindset: don’t chase, let price come to you. If bonds (HYG, LQD) start breaking down, that's your cue to reduce risk immediately, but for now, they're merely approaching — not confirmed.
callout_body:
SPY: $680.19 (20 MA) — daily close above needed for momentum
XLF: $52.60 (200 MA) — canary in coal mine, risk elevated
action_items: Rotate into Energy (XLE), Materials (XLB), and Agriculture (MOO, ADM) on pullbacks above 200 MA; avoid lagging Healthcare and Financials.
Watch SPY and QQQ for daily closes above 20 MA; until then, stay defensive and avoid chasing tech names below their MAs.
closing_quote: If everyone's panicking, that's when you start shopping — but only if the flows agree.
